steps.title: Write the width in terms of $x$
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the length, in inches. The length is $24$ inches longer than the width, so:
$$\text{width} = x - 24.$$

steps.title: Apply the area formula
steps.content:
Area $=$ length $\times$ width $= 2{,}661$:
$$x(x - 24) = 2{,}661.$$
Expand: $x^{2} - 24x = 2{,}661$, then move everything to one side:
$$x^{2} - 24x - 2{,}661 = 0.$$

steps.title: Compare to each choice
steps.content:
• $0={x}^{2}-24x-2,661$: $0 = x^{2} - 24x - 2{,}661$: matches the derived equation exactly.
  • $0={x}^{2}-24x+2,661$: $0 = x^{2} - 24x + 2{,}661$: wrong sign on the constant; would mean $x(x-24) = -2{,}661$.
  • $0={x}^{2}+24x-2,661$: $0 = x^{2} + 24x - 2{,}661$: would correspond to width $= x + 24$, which contradicts "length is longer than width".
  • $0={x}^{2}+24x+2,661$: $0 = x^{2} + 24x + 2{,}661$: discriminant $24^{2} - 4(2{,}661) \lt 0$, no real solution, so no valid length.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
